card.intuition: The 3-layered framework for measuring reliability: SLIs (What we measure), SLOs (Our target), and SLAs (Our legal promise to the customer).
card.analogy: A pizza delivery: **SLI** is the stopwatch showing '35 minutes'. **SLO** is the chef's goal: 'Keep 99% of deliveries under 40 mins'. **SLA** is the flyer: 'If it's more than 60 mins, you get a free pizza'.
when_to_use: Communicating the quality of your service to paid customers (SLA)
Setting internal engineering priorities (if we miss an SLO, stop building features and fix bugs)
Measuring technical success in SRE (Site Reliability Engineering)
gotchas: 100% is impossible: don't promise 100% uptime. Even Google only promises 'Five Nines' (99.999%), which still allows 5 minutes of downtime per year.
The 'Error Budget': if your SLO is 99%, you have 1% 'Budget' to break things while experimenting.

exercise.prompt: Identify the acronym for the actual METRIC being measured (e.g. latency or error rate).
hints: Three letters, starts with S, ends with I
exercise.test_code:
def check(ans): return ans.upper() == "SLI"
assert check("SLI")
print("PASS")
